description: Tunable parameters and decision order for bore/boss/plane/slot classification. Mirrors constants and logic documented in 3. classify_features.py as of this date. The Python module remains authoritative until load_rule_sheet() (or equivalent) is implemented.
decision_priority: seed_type branches: background → background; plane/plane_feature → pocket vs planar_face; slot → slot; boss → boss; bore → bore tree
bore: len(radii) >= 2 → counterbore vs large_bore (max/min radius vs thresholds)
bore: single radius, radius >= large_bore_radius_mm → large_bore
bore: single face → ddr = depth / (2*radius); ddr <= single_face_through_hole_ddr_max → through_hole (medium confidence) else blind_hole (low)
append _angled when is_principal_axis is false (except unknown_bore)

large_bore_radius_mm.role: Radius above which a single-step bore is large_bore; also used in multi-radius bore max-radius check.

single_face_through_hole_ddr_max.role: If depth / (2*r) <= this, treat single-face bore as through_hole (thin arc / exit not in cluster).

pocket_max_area_mm2.role: Plane cluster with perpendicular walls must have floor area below this to be pocket vs planar_face.

drill_max_radius_mm.role: If smallest radius in a multi-radius bore >= this, classify whole feature as large_bore (cannot drill smallest step).

pocket_rules.notes: If has_perpendicular_walls and perp_wall_count >= min_perpendicular_wall_count and face_area < pocket_max_area_mm2 → pocket (or pocket_angled).

warnings: chamfer and tapped_hole are not produced by classify_cluster() today; MFCAD supervision uses 07_label_taxonomy.json.
large_bore_angled is produced by classifier but select_process() only branches on 'large_bore' without suffix — verify dispatcher if angled large bores appear.
